observations.recommendation: Management to: Require the Municipal Engineer to determine whether the unidentified roads (see Appendix D) are municipal roads. From the result thereof, prepare a listing of municipal roads, revised the road map, and furnish the Municipal Treasurer/Inventory Committee; Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count on said listing and prepare the RPCLRN and submit to the Accounting Unit and to our Office; Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain the equivalent LRNPC and include in its Local Road Networks Inventory. Also, with the coordination of the Municipal Engineer, prepare the necessary documents to facilitate the transfer/turn-over of road projects under the jurisdiction of the DPWH, Province of Zamboanga del Norte and Barangay, if there are any, as basis for the Municipal Accountant to drop these road networks from the books of accounts; and Instruct the Municipal Accountant to determine the difference of P118,161.30 per RPCLR for RCBC-Ocupe Street and make necessary adjustments, if applicable. Also, based on the transfer/turn-over documents, make necessary adjustments to drop these road networks from the books of accounts.

Management to: Require the Municipal Treasurer with the coordination of the Municipal Engineer to prepare the necessary documents to transfer/turn-over those roads not identified as municipal roads to the concerned agencies. These documents will be the basis of the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments to drop these assets from the books of accounts. The adjusting entries are as follows: Government Equity P21,581,375.69 Road Networks P21,581,375.69 To recognize transferred/turned-over road projects to the DPWH, Province of Zamboanga del Norte and Barangay, respectively. Moreover, direct the Municipal accountant to require the barangay bookkeeper to record these road projects in the barangay books of accounts.

Management to: Require the Municipal Engineer to identify the road lot component of each local road, its area measurement and fair value with the coordination of the Municipal Assessor, and submit to the Municipal Accountant; Direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to recognize these road lots in the books of accounts as part of Road Networks and update the corresponding LRNLC; and, Locate/secure the donation papers of the donated lot for the transfer of its ownership in the LGU’s name.

Management to: Municipal Accountant To record purchases in bulk or carried on stock to its appropriate Inventory account on the basis of Delivery Receipts and Acceptance and Inspection Report (AIR); and To record expenses on the basis of the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) together with the supporting RIS. Requisitioning Office To prepare and submit RIS with its supporting documents to the Supply Officer. Supply Officer To consolidate the RIS in the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued and submit to the Accounting Unit for the recognition of issuance under its appropriate Expenses account.

Management to: 1.Require the BAC Secretariat to submit all enumerated lacking documents stated in Appendix E and to ensure that necessary procurement documents are prepared, complied and duly accomplished; 2.Direct the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly check all supporting documents to ensure that the required supporting documents are appropriate, properly accomplished and completely attached to the disbursement vouchers.

The management to: prioritize the creation of four (4) mandatory positions to elevate the organizational structure and staffing pattern of the LDRRMO, in accordance with JMC 2014-1. Also, require the finance committee to come up with a proposal as to its funding source, either decreasing the appropriations of other expenses or discontinuing those benefits which are not mandatory.

The management to inform our Office within 10 days after the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted in order for our Office to validate the same.

The management to furnish the Auditor with the required copies of contract documents/purchase/letter/job orders and their supporting documents as well as notice of deliveries within the prescribed period for the timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same.

We recommended that management:


     a. Direct the Inventory Committee to prepare a Physical Inventory Plan
        (PIP) and submit the same to the COA Audit Team for the  subsequent
        physical count at least ten  calendar  days  before  the  scheduled
        start of inventory taking activities, together with the  Invitation
        Letter to witness the entire inventory taking, in  compliance  with
        the pertinent provisions of Section 5 of said Circular;

     b. Require the Inventory Committee  to  record/document  the  physical
        count daily using the ICF per PPE sub-major account group and will
        be the basis in the preparation of RPCPPE, List  of  PPE  Found  at
        Station and List of Non-Existing/Missing PPEs;

     c. Inform the Inventory Committee to submit the RPCPPE as of  December
        31, 2021 to the Audit Team;


     d. Direct the Property Unit to prepare a List of PPEs Found at Station
        (Annex B of said Circular) and List  of  Non-Existing/Missing  PPEs
        (Annex C of said Circular) during  physical  inventory  and  submit
        these lists to the Accounting Unit for recording in  the  books  of
        accounts  of  the  LGU  to  establish  the  existence  and   proper
        accountability of the PPE accounts; and

     e. Ensure that  the  guidelines  and  procedures  in  the  conduct  of
        physical count of PPE as provided  in  COA  Circular  2020-006  are
        strictly observed to  facilitate  the  one-time  cleansing  of  PPE
        account balances.

Management to direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments to correct the erroneous recording of the above items. Also, require the Municipal Accountant to account items which are capital in nature under PPE account, regardless of the sources of charges. Moreover, direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) of the remaining 18 units stand fan to support transfer of accountability to the end-user.

The management to require the Municipal Treasurer to monitor its Collecting Officers and instruct them to strictly issue official receipts in numerical sequence by issuing the first series of stub and then the next, for easy monitoring of collections and consequently the reconciliation of AFs received and issued at the end of the day, as required under Section 73 of Article II of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM).

The management to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to require the Collecting Officer to remit collections regularly and intact. Also, constantly monitor the remittance of its collections to ensure that remittances are made in accordance with Section 29 of the NGAS in order to avoid possible loss or robbery and/or misappropriation or misuse of government funds.

The management to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to direct the Collecting Officers to prepare and submit to him the required Report of Accountability for Accountable Form on a monthly basis in conformity with the above-cited regulations in order to facilitate the preparation and submission of Consolidated RAAF to our Office.

The management to direct the accounting unit to update and maintain subsidiary ledger for the collections and remittances of said collecting officer to facilitate in the reconciliation of his accountabilities and to determine his correct accountabilities at a given period. Also, require the hiring of additional accounting personnel to be responsible for the preparation of subsidiary ledger.

The management to observe the proper turnover of accountabilities between one collecting officer to another collecting officer through the accomplishment of invoice and receipt and affixing their signature in the accountable form control book as proof that these accountable forms were received by them. Moreover, direct the Municipal Treasurer to accomplish the Invoice and Receipt for the issuance of accountable forms carried in stock in compliance with Section 123, Volume I of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs.

The management to: prepare Purchase Request in accordance with the period indicated in the Project Procurement Management Plans (PPMPs) to avoid advance withdrawals of diesel. Moreover, stop the practice of allowing advance withdrawals of diesel as this scheme is considered inappropriate procurement procedures under R.A. 9184 giving unwarranted benefits to the supplier whose goods were already used without the benefit of a valid contract. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly check the supporting documents to ensure that the dates of the attached Driver’s Ticket and Delivery Receipts are after the date of the receipt of PO. Those dates ahead of the date of the receipt of PO are to be excluded from the payment. Also, require the BAC to reflect the actual agreement of deliveries/withdrawals in the PO. Also, assigned personnel responsible for the monitoring of fuel withdrawals, preparation of Fuel Consumption Report and the preparation of Purchase Request.
The management to closely monitor the offices responsible for its implementation to pursue the timely and full implementation of all projects/programs, as planned.
The management to require the Market Committee to set guidelines on the duration of the Contract of Lease to ensure fairness and equality among Lessees.

The management to require the Municipal Market Committee and the Market Supervisor/Inspector to take action on the above violations by imposing surcharges/penalties or declaring the stall/booth vacant in order to protect the LGU’s interest. Also, direct the Market Supervisor/Inspector to continuously monitor the occupied units to prevent the Lessee from sub-leasing, selling its right over the unit or permitting other person to conduct business therein. Moreover, direct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly impose the daily/monthly rentals of the stalls/booths, in accordance with Section 12 of Article II and Section 1 of Article III, Chapter V of said Tax Ordinance.
The management to require the collectors to collect these underpaid taxes upon their succeeding payment of taxes due (see Appendix L).

Moreover, direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact all his collections including those turned-over to him by the collectors with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day.

We recommend management to require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count on all its local road networks in order to ascertain the existence of all recorded Road Networks.
Likewise, require the Municipal Treasurer being the General Services Officer to prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Property Cards for all roads and its components, including Road Map. A Report on Local Road Network is to be prepared also at the end of accounting period.
Moreover, require the Municipal Accountant to maintain Local Road Network Ledger Card using Annex D of COA Circular No. 2015-008. Likewise, provide depreciation expenses on its Road Networks and prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for the current and previous years.

We recommend management to require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count on all its local road networks in order to ascertain the existence of all recorded Road Networks.

Likewise, the remaining 25% are to be recognized in the books at the end of CY 2019.

We recommend that management:
Stop the practice of recognizing accounts payable based on the obligations per Municipal Budget Office records. Liability is only recognized when goods and services are accepted or rendered and supplier/creditor bills are received.
Require the attachment of documents necessary to prove the existence and validity of the obligations/accounts payable recognized.
Submit the corresponding Payrolls/Official Receipts/Delivery Receipts/Inspection and Acceptance Report/Billing Statements in order for this Office to validate the recognized accounts payable totaling to P24,219,937.84.
Effect the necessary adjustments for accounts payable without proper documentations/valid obligations
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant take-up the necessary adjustments/corrections of the affected accounts to provide accurate and reliable financial information.

Require all concerned officials and employees to liquidate and/or settle immediately their outstanding cash advances. Likewise, require the officers and employees with unexpended balance of cash advances to immediately refund.
Also, send follow-up and/or demand letters requiring the personnel concerned to liquidate their respective cash advances, otherwise, impose the withholding of their salaries until full liquidation/settlement are made, as required under Section 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002. For those who are no longer connected with the Municipality, demand letters are to be sent again to their present addresses or new official stations.

We recommend management to instruct the concerned collector to impose penalty of 2% for every month of delinquency instead of a discount if the total amount due for the year is paid after the 1st quarter of same year, in consonance with Sections 250 and 255 of RA 7160. Also, impose the appropriate penalties for late payments of taxes due.
Moreover, require the collectors to collect these underpaid taxes upon their succeeding payment of taxes due (see Appendix L).

We recommend management to immediately remit the unremitted shares of various barangays from RPT and CTC. Also, effect the timely release of the shares of the barangays from basic RPT and CTC as required under Section 271 (d) of RA 7160.

We recommend management to ensure that utilization of the 20% DF is within the allowable development projects enumerated in DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1.  Also, require that programs and activities which are not considered as development projects be included in their respective appropriate budget.

We recommend management to prepare the ObR based on the PO/Contract and/or upon payment to ensure that only valid obligations were recorded in the RAAO. Also, include the amount of P152,479.20 as part of the unobligated balance of 20% DF for CY 2018.

We recommend that the management prioritize the creation of these four (4) mandatory positions to elevate the organizational structure and staffing pattern of the LDRRMO, in accordance with JMC 2014-1.

We recommend management to:
Notify the homeowners and establishments to segregate their respective solid wastes before bringing them to the designated area for on-site collections.
Monitor the personnel in the dumpsite/sanitary landfill area to ensure that open burning is not carried out.
Strictly impose fines and penalties to any violation of the prohibitive provisions of said Municipal Ordinance in order to encourage the community to act in accordance with said Ordinance; thus, minimizing violators.

We recommend management to assign personnel to be responsible for the following duties:
Inclusion of high value properties to insurance coverage.
Extraction from the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment, the high value properties subject to insurance.
Appraisal of the insurable properties.
Preparation and submission of Property Inventory Form (PIF) listing of all the insurable properties and other assets, showing their latest appraised values/valuation, appraisal date, location, and other information using the form provided in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2018-002 to COA and to GIF, GSIS, not later than April 30 of each year.
Inclusion in the LGU Annual Budget the amount of premiums for the general insurance covering all insurable properties and ensure its payment to the GSIS.

We recommend management to exclude RATA in the computation of the total allowable tax exemption. RATA is not subject to income as provided under Section 7 of RR No. 11-2018. Also, file a claim for credit or refund with the BIR to recover the taxes erroneously computed, withheld and remitted in CY 2018.

We recommend management to direct the Municipal Treasurer to set schedule of collection remittances and require the collectors to remit their collections on the scheduled date. In case of failure of the Collector to report and remit his collections on the scheduled date, require the return of unissued accountable forms in his possession to avoid further accumulation of unaccounted collections and juggling of subsequent collections. 

Likewise, subsequent issuance of accountable forms to the collectors is to be made in quantities that are necessary and only when the collections on previous issued accountable forms have been reported/remitted. 

Moreover, direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact all his collections including those turned-over to him by the collectors with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day. Further, direct the Municipal Treasurer to religiously check the statement of accountable forms as to initial balances on hand, receipts, issues and the ending balances on hand and to make a physical count of the accountable forms remaining in the custody of the collector and check the same against the new balances on hand column.

observations.reason: The scheduled date for remittances was not religiously being followed by the collectors. Likewise, there were still instances where deposits were not made in the next banking day.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend management to direct the Bids and Awards Committee to thoroughly examine the documents submitted by the bidders to ensure that all required data/information are complete and will serve its purpose.    
In the event that the opening of bids has to be postponed, require the BAC Chairman to issue Notice of Postponement indicating thereat the rescheduled date and time of the opening of bids. The Notice of Postponement should be posted in the PhilGEPs website and the website of the LGU-Polanco. Likewise, notify immediately our Office and the 2 observers of the change in schedule. 

Also, direct the BAC Secretary to ensure that the invitation to witness the opening of bids should be received by our Office and the concerned observers at least 5 calendar days before the opening of bids. 

Moreover, the Checklist of Eligibility Requirements for the BAC should indicate the actual date of opening of first envelope. Likewise, Abstract of Bids should indicate the complete date and time of the opening of bids. 

On the other hand, require the supplier to post the performance security within 10 days after the receipt of the Notice of Award. The Purchase Order is to be prepared by the BAC only after the supplier has posted the required performance security. Also, the Purchase Order should indicate the date and term of delivery. This will be used as basis for the computation of the liquidated damages, if any. In case that the procured items are perishable and impractical to be delivered one time, the date and term of delivery should be indicated in the bidding documents and eventually to the Purchase Order to avoid wastage of goods. Moreover, the date of delivery should be realistic and in consonance with the period of the program/project.
observations.managementAction: Bidders in subsequent procurements were thoroughly checked by the BAC. Likewise, Invitation Letters to observations and Notices of Postponement were issued within the prescribed period. The BAC Secretary was also instructed to strictly monitor the posting of performance security before the execution of the contract between the LGU and the winning bidder.

      Require all concerned officials  and  employees  to  liquidate  and/or
settle immediately their outstanding cash advances.  Likewise,  require  the
officers  and  employees  with  unexpended  balance  of  cash  advances   to
immediately refund.


      Also, send follow-up and/or demand  letters  requiring  the  personnel
concerned to liquidate their respective  cash  advances,  otherwise,  impose
the withholding of their  salaries  until  full  liquidation/settlement  are
made, as required under Section 5.9 of COA Circular No.  97-002.  For  those
who are no longer connected with the Municipality, demand letters are to  be
sent again to their present addresses or new official stations.

Recommendation

      We recommend management to limit the granting of cash advances to  the
Disbursing Officer within the allowable maximum cash accountability  covered
by her bond pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 and  Treasury  Circular  No.
02-2009. In no  case  shall  the  total  accountability  of  the  Disbursing
Officer exceed the maximum cash accountability covered by her bond.

      Moreover, require the Municipal Accountant to ensure  that  additional
bond is applied for in case cash accountability is increased.

Recommendation

      We   recommend management to create another Inventory Committee  since
the existing Inventory Committee continuously  failed  to  conduct  physical
inventory of all property, plant and equipment of  the  LGU.  The  Inventory
Committee is to be composed of  members  who  can  actually  allot  time  to
conduct  the  said  physical  count,  and  to   prepare   and   submit   the
corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and  Equipment
(RPCPPE) to the  Office  of  the  Auditor,  in  accordance  with  the  above
provision.

      To expedite the conduct of physical count of all of its  PPE,  require
the Inventory Committee to furnish each office a list  of  their  respective
PPE and to present these PPE during the scheduled date of inventory.

      Moreover, require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain  property  cards
for property, plant and equipment.

Recommendation

      We recommend management to direct the Bids  and  Awards  Committee  to
thoroughly examine the documents submitted by the  bidders  to  ensure  that
all required data/information are complete and will serve its purpose.

      In the event that the opening of bids has to be postponed, require the
BAC  Chairman  to  issue  Notice  of  Postponement  indicating  thereat  the
rescheduled  date  and  time  of  the  opening  of  bids.  The   Notice   of
Postponement should be posted in the PhilGEPs website  and  the  website  of
the  LGU-Polanco.  Likewise,  notify  immediately  our  Office  and  the   2
observers of the change in schedule.


      Also, direct the BAC  Secretary  to  ensure  that  the  invitation  to
witness the opening of bids  should  be  received  by  our  Office  and  the
concerned observers at least 5 calendar days before the opening of bids.


      Moreover, the Checklist of Eligibility Requirements for the BAC should
indicate the actual date of opening of first  envelope.  Likewise,  Abstract
of Bids should indicate the complete date and time of the opening of bids.


      On the other hand,  require  the  supplier  to  post  the  performance
security within 10 days after the  receipt  of  the  Notice  of  Award.  The
Purchase Order is to be prepared by the BAC  only  after  the  supplier  has
posted the required performance security. Also, the  Purchase  Order  should
indicate the date and term of delivery. This will be used as basis  for  the
computation of the liquidated damages, if any. In  case  that  the  procured
items are perishable and impractical to be delivered one time, the date  and
term  of  delivery  should  be  indicated  in  the  bidding  documents   and
eventually to the Purchase Order to avoid wastage of  goods.  Moreover,  the
date of delivery should be realistic and in consonance with  the  period  of
the program/project.

Recommendation

      We recommend management to issue Notice of Award within  the  prescribed
period. Also, ensure that no  workers  are  listed  as  employed  in  different projects at the same time.


      Moreover, require the withholding  of  retention  money  for  progress
billing payment as required under the Pakyaw Agreement.  However,  if  found
to be unnecessary, require the omission of this clause in subsequent  Pakyaw
Agreement.

             Further,  require  the  Municipal   Treasurer   and   Municipal
Accountant to monitor the availability of funds before  certifying  the  box
for funds availability in the disbursement voucher and request for  payment,
respectively, to avoid disbursement of funds more  than  what  is  available
and/or resorting to using other funds intended for another purposes.

Recommendation

      We recommend management to ensure that utilization of the  20%  DF  is
within the allowable development projects enumerated  in  DILG-DBM  JMC  No.
2017-1.  Also,  require  that  projects/programs/activities  which  are  not
considered  as  development  projects  be  included  in   their   respective
appropriate budget.

Recommendation


      We recommend management to direct the Municipal Solid Waste Management
Board to immediately prepare and submit to the Commission the  revised  Plan
for the approval of the LGU’s 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan  from  the
Commission.
